data.title: Aerodrome Public Product Fund has repurchased 1.5 million AERO and locked them as veAERO
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Aerodrome announced that its Public Product Fund (PGF) has repurchased 1.5 million AERO and locked them as veAERO to increase PGF voting rights.

data.title: Rebecca Simmonds appointed new CEO of Walrus Foundation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Decentralized storage and data availability protocol Walrus announced on X that Rebecca Simmonds (@rj_simmonds) has become the new CEO of the Walrus Foundation. As the Web3 storage field heats up and the mainnet is about to be launched, she will help Walrus expand, innovate, and break boundaries.

data.title: Burwick Law, the law firm that sued pump.fun, asked the platform to remove tokens that used its IP
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News US law firm Burwick Law said in a statement on February 5 that it and Wolf Popper had sent a cease and desist letter to pump.fun, demanding the "immediate removal" of Dog Shit Going NoWhere (DOGSHIT2) and other tokens that "impersonated our company" by using intellectual property (including its logo and name) without permission.
According to pump.fun, multiple users created tokens using the names and logos of Burwick Law and Wolf Popper in various configurations. In the ongoing lawsuit against pump.fun, some tokens also used the names and portraits of Burwick Law employees and one of its clients.
The law firm said: "Burwick Law confirmed that pump.fun has the technical ability to delete these tokens, but chose not to take action, despite the obvious financial and legal risks to the public."
Earlier in January, Burwick Law, a US law firm specializing in the field of cryptocurrency, said it was considering filing a lawsuit on behalf of investors who suffered losses from pump.fun.
Burwick said the move came after "months" of working with ordinary people who had lost "significant amounts of money" to unfulfilled promises on platforms such as Meme Coin, Rug Pull and pump.fun. The statement added: "Over the past few months, pump.fun has collected hundreds of millions of dollars in fees while the platform has been rife with illegal drug use, self-harm, racism, anti-Semitism, obscenity, violence and other antisocial behavior."

data.title: Ethereum Gas Fees Drop to New Low
data.description: According to Foresight News, data from Etherscan indicates that Ethereum gas fees have decreased to 1.039 Gwei today. The estimated cost for a swap transaction is currently $0.91.

data.title: ETH block options on Deribit buys put and call options at $2,600 at the end of June
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Lin Chen, head of Asia-Pacific business at Deribit, wrote on X that today’s largest ETH bulk option transaction was: a user paid $876,000 to purchase $2,600 put and call options at the end of June, for a total of 1,000 ETH on one side.

data.title: Aptos launches blockchain execution engine Shardines, which can achieve over one million TPS
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Aptos Labs announced the launch of Shardines, the next generation of blockchain execution engines designed to achieve horizontal scalability and more than 1 million TPS.
According to reports, the blockchain execution engine can be expanded independently of consensus and storage. This enables validator operators to optimize resources and enhance system flexibility. Shardines uses a dynamic partitioner to minimize cross-shard network communication and waiting time to adapt to changing workloads. The innovative "micro-batching and pipelining" strategy further mitigates the impact of network latency by amortizing costs, thereby ensuring high performance.
In addition, the team has open-sourced Shardines' fully reproducible benchmarks for blockchain enthusiasts to verify the results.
